description: Lock the 2026-06-27 advanced_metallurgy_medieval correction that scopes the node to broad medieval ferrous process improvement rather than modern scientific metallurgy or a bronze-working dependency.

checks.reason: The node remains a broad early-medieval ferrous-smithing umbrella with explicit date uncertainty; later blast-furnace and armor-plate maturity appears downstream and in source locators.
Iron working is the direct process base for bloomery consolidation, forge welding, carburized steel, and medieval ferrous heat treatment.
Ore supply and preparation remain enabling inputs for medieval iron and steel production.
Bronze working is retained only as earlier metallurgical craft lineage, not as a direct ferrous process dependency.
Empirical carbon, oxidation/reduction, slag, and heat control are historical craft chemistry context rather than modern chemical theory.
